data.description:
## Why
Tier B of the bash->Python strangler-fig (story adult-oxide-slave, docs/bash-migration.md §4). Port leaf-write command bodies out of bash (ticket-lib-api.sh / ticket-*.sh) into rebar._commands, behind the REBAR_LEAF_WRITES kill-switch, reusing the existing rebar._engine_support.resolver and the event-append seam. Default stays bash until parity-green + dogfood soak.

## What
- Foundation: ticket-append-event.sh seam (one locked write path wrapping write_commit_event), rebar._switch (REBAR_LEAF_WRITES parsing, REBAR_PUSH idiom), rebar._commands package.
- Per command (comment, tag/untag, set-file-impact, set-verify-commands, archive, then create/edit/link/unlink/revert): characterize -> port -> switch-in at dispatcher + library/MCP -> dual-run the bash suite against BOTH impls byte-identically.

## Scope
Leaf writes only; transition/claim/reopen keep ticket_txn.py. No store/schema/lock-design change. Flip/soak/retire are later, separate-release steps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ticket-append-event.sh seam in place; all Python writes route through it (I5 single locked write path preserved)
- [ ] rebar._switch resolves REBAR_LEAF_WRITES with the REBAR_PUSH idiom (case-insensitive, default bash, typo-tolerant)
- [ ] Each leaf command ported to rebar._commands and switch-wired at dispatcher + library
- [ ] Each command's bash suite passes against REBAR_LEAF_WRITES=bash AND =python (dual-run parity)
- [ ] Default remains bash
data.title: Tier B: leaf-write commands ported to rebar._commands behind REBAR_LEAF_WRITES
